description: Lock Agricultural Revolution & Enclosures to enclosure/land-tenure consolidation, crop-rotation intensification, and selective livestock breeding rather than mercantilist theory or ancient seed-saving background.

checks.reason: Improved rotations build from earlier rotation and fallow practice.
Crop-rotation practice remains an enabling agronomic base for the later revolution.
The source-backed selective-breeding component is livestock breeding.
Selective animal breeding enables the livestock-improvement component without covering enclosure as a whole.
Enclosure depends on boundary and tenure consolidation.
Land-tenure boundary practice enables enclosure without being identical to the whole agricultural revolution.
Ancient seed-saving is broader plant-domestication background, not the source-backed livestock-breeding component.
Mercantilism is a broad state trade theory and not a direct prerequisite for enclosure.
The node remains anchored to the 18th-century British transformation at century precision.
